anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Successful dual-trained practitioners who move between traditions with different final aims without predictable friction, or switchers whose failures track teacher fit, trauma history, retreat intensity, or technique mismatch rather than prior telos.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Keeps the idea below candidate discovery. The anomaly search lowers confidence in grain as a real independent variable, but supports the stricter surplus test as a useful guardrail.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If difficulty follows ordinary transfer distance, social ecology, or clinical risk factors, then telos-bearing residue adds no explanatory power. If incompatible teloi coexist smoothly, grain may be an artifact of poor pedagogy or identity conflict.

claim_units: Contemplative skills may carry directional residue from a prior path's final aim.
Procedural inertia is a rival explanation and must be controlled before invoking grain.
Claimant or transfer grammar is a second rival explanation and must be controlled before invoking grain.
Cross-training difficulty can be decomposed into procedural residue, claimant-grammar conflict, and telos-bearing residue.
Grain is the surplus directional residue left after ordinary skill transfer and practice ecology are accounted for.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Professional retraining, such as a litigator becoming a mediator or a classical musician becoming a jazz improviser.
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Retraining failures will be best explained by old success criterion only after task habit and institutional role grammar are controlled. The former litigator should struggle not only from adversarial tactics but from a residual win condition that organizes attention even after mediation procedures are learned.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Old dissolution target becomes old success criterion. Procedural residue becomes practiced operation. Claimant grammar becomes the new field's rules for who may act, decide, claim competence, or count success.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If retraining difficulty is fully explained by task habits, incentives, identity, and organizational norms, with no residual effect from old success criteria, the surplus-grain structure weakens.

exact_claim: A contemplative capacity should be called grained only when the prior dissolution target predicts transfer difficulty better than repeated operation and the receiving practice's transfer grammar. Cross-training failures should be coded as procedural residue, claimant-grammar conflict, and telos-bearing residue.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Blind coders can code a practitioner's prior telos from manuals or teacher interviews, then predict specific later transfer frictions beyond technique similarity and agency grammar.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Prior dissolution target has independent predictive value after operation and grammar are controlled.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Mixed-method cross-training study with preregistered coding and qualitative comparative analysis.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Old telos has no independent effect once task similarity, teacher relation, social identity, dosage, mental health, and receiving doctrine are included.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Same-technique different-grammar cases produce different failures than different-technique same-grammar cases, and telos-shift cases show an additional asymmetric pattern.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Procedural residue, claimant-grammar conflict, and telos-bearing residue are separable layers.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Anonymized practitioner narratives coded by independent contemplative teachers and researchers.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Inter-rater reliability fails, or all reported failures collapse into familiar categories such as habit, confusion, teacher mismatch, or motivation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Onboarding that explicitly reframes final aim, ownership, verification, and repair reduces predictable switching errors more than technical instruction alone.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Retargeting a practice requires more than teaching the new operation.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Prospective comparison of cross-training onboarding protocols.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Telos reframing adds no improvement beyond rapport, safety screening, practice dosage, and community support.

literature_search_queries: transfer of training identical elements Thorndike Woodworth
Barnett Ceci taxonomy far transfer content context functional social context
Baldwin Ford transfer of training trainee characteristics training design work environment
Pierre Hadot spiritual exercises philosophy as a way of life schools practices
Steven Katz Language Epistemology and Mysticism no pure experience
George Lindbeck doctrine as grammar rules discourse attitude action
Pargament religion problem solving process three styles coping self directing deferring collaborative
Varieties of Contemplative Experience meditation challenges practice practitioner relationship context
Buddhist upaya skillful means capacity pedagogy
Dogen Bendowa practice realization zazen primary text

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate treats identical-elements transfer as a control variable, not the whole explanation, then asks whether prior telos adds predictive value.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large reduction for the procedural-inertia layer.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for procedural residue: prior learning transfers or interferes depending on shared elements between old and new tasks.
near_neighbors.source: Thorndike and Woodworth identical-elements transfer theory, discussed in Cox, https://doi.org/10.1207/s15326985ep3201_4

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds religious final aim and claimability grammar as domain-specific transfer dimensions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The research design resembles existing transfer taxonomy.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for decomposing transfer by content and context, including functional and social context.
near_neighbors.source: Barnett and Ceci, When and Where Do We Apply What We Learn?, https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2002-00742-005

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate would need to show that telos-bearing residue predicts outcomes after these standard transfer factors are coded.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to large reduction. Many proposed controls already exist in training-transfer research.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for predicting transfer from training design, trainee characteristics, and work environment.
near_neighbors.source: Baldwin and Ford transfer-of-training model, summarized in Govaerts, Kyndt and Dochy, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12186-017-9190-y

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate narrows Hadot's school-shaped exercise insight into a residual transfer test across contemplative traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large reduction for the broad claim that practices are not neutral techniques.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for exercises being embedded in a way of life, a master-student relation, repeated disciplines, and a transformed way of perceiving.
near_neighbors.source: Pierre Hadot on spiritual exercises, Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://iep.utm.edu/hadot/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate is weaker metaphysically but more operational: it tests transfer friction rather than claiming experience is wholly constructed.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large reduction for anti-neutrality. Remaining novelty is methodological.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for tradition-shaped experience and the rejection of context-free mystical practice or perception.
near_neighbors.source: Steven Katz and radical contextualism in mystical experience studies, summarized in https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/13/10/983

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate applies grammar specifically as a rival predictor of cross-training failure before telos residue may be counted.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for doctrine functioning as communal rules of discourse, attitude, and action.
near_neighbors.source: George Lindbeck's cultural-linguistic doctrine-as-grammar model, discussed in https://repository.up.ac.za/bitstream/handle/2263/64227/Kim_Study_2018.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate treats agency allocation as one layer in a broader transfer-failure model rather than as the primary typology.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction, especially for claimant and support-locus claims.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for agency allocation between practitioner and sacred source, especially self-directing, deferring, and collaborative styles.
near_neighbors.source: Pargament et al., Religion and the Problem-Solving Process: Three Styles of Coping, https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate predicts a narrower cause of cross-training difficulty and must beat VCE-style practice, practitioner, relationship, and context factors.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. VCE is a strong baseline.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for qualitative coding of meditation-related difficulties, interpretations, responses, and influencing factors.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl, Fisher, Cooper, Rosen and Britton, Varieties of Contemplative Experience project, https://sites.brown.edu/britton/research/the-varieties-of-contemplative-experience/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate asks when prior capacity itself has directional residue, not only how teachers adapt methods to current capacity.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. Pedagogical adaptation is old.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for adapting methods to capacity, stage, and context rather than treating a technique as universally portable.
near_neighbors.source: Buddhist upaya or skillful means, Encyclopedia.com, https://www.encyclopedia.com/religion/encyclopedias-almanacs-transcripts-and-maps/upaya

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds procedural inertia as an explicit control and makes grain a residual claim rather than a general feature.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High internal reduction. Treat as refinement and test protocol, not standalone discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close for capacity direction, handoff timing, support locus, forbidden claimant, and claimability stance.
near_neighbors.source: Prior Lumenary records supplied in the prompt: Grain of Capacity, Handoff Point, Capacity Ledger, Claimability and Support Rubric

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as doctrine. Treat as a research-control rule for cross-training studies.

Build a preregistered coding sheet for old operation, old dissolution target, receiving claimant grammar, teacher relation, community ecology, identity investment, mental health factors, and failure mode.
Use split-source blind coding: code prior telos from old manuals before reading outcomes, then code receiving grammar from new manuals before reading repairs.
Compare predictive value against transfer theory, situated learning, Hadot, Katz, Sharf, Lindbeck, Pargament, upaya, VCE, and prior Lumenary ledgers.
Use successful dual practice, inherited religion, radical other-power, and traditions that reject transfer accounting as anomaly classes.
Require inter-rater reliability before using telos-bearing residue publicly.
Interview practitioners for concrete switching failures and repairs, not abstract agreement.
Do not raise above novel_synthesis unless old telos predicts failure and repair patterns better than technique, receiving grammar, social ecology, and clinician-coded risk factors.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.69 to about 0.62. Procedural transfer, practice ecology, doctrine as grammar, agency allocation, tradition-conditioned experience, and capacity-sensitive pedagogy are well anticipated. The remaining contribution is a stricter residual test for prior Lumenary grain claims.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is ordinary skill transfer, lineage pedagogy, upaya, spiritual direction, trauma-informed screening, or religious coping. They cannot identify telos-specific repairs beyond familiar cautions about pride, impatience, confusion, or poor fit.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners independently report cases where identical techniques fail differently because the student's previous final aim changes what they think the practice is doing, and they can name repairs that specifically retarget final aim rather than only technique or agency language.
questions: When students arrive from another tradition, can you distinguish old technique habits from old ownership or authority grammar?
Do some students bring a prior final aim that bends how they understand your practice even after learning the correct technique?
Can you give one concrete failure case where telos explains the problem better than technique, teacher fit, trauma, temperament, or community mismatch?
Does this three-layer model change how you repair the student's practice?
Are there traditions or students for whom the transfer framing itself distorts the situation?
relevant_practitioners: Theravada teachers familiar with right effort and anatta
Soto Zen teachers familiar with practice-realization
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers familiar with direct introduction
Advaita Vedanta teachers familiar with self-inquiry
Contemplative clinicians familiar with meditation-related difficulties

primary_pair: Dogen, Bendowa, Tanahashi translation excerpt and commentary, https://everydayzen.org/teachings/dogens-bendowa-part-1-of-3/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. Telos-bearing residue may be a residual bucket for variables already covered by technique, teacher relation, community, social identity, trauma history, motivation, and doctrinal language. The model needs strict stopping rules so unexplained difficulty is not automatically called grain.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The search found strong near-neighbors in transfer-of-training theory, Barnett and Ceci's far-transfer taxonomy, Hadot on school-shaped spiritual exercises, Katz on tradition-conditioned experience, Lindbeck on doctrine as grammar, Pargament on agency allocation, situated learning, Buddhist upaya, and VCE adverse-effects coding. It did not yet close-read full books by Hadot, Katz, Sharf, Lindbeck, Lave and Wenger, or Pargament, and it did not code actual cross-training cases.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 presents path factors as trainable functions, including right view, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion. Dogen's Bendowa makes zazen not merely one transferable technique but the expression of the Way itself, where practice and realization are not cleanly separated. The pair supports the candidate's distinction between repeated operation and practice grammar. Similar sitting or effort can have different meaning, authority, and endpoint structures.

title: The Surplus Grain Test
unlike_statement: Unlike transfer theory, Hadot, Katz, Lindbeck, Pargament, upaya, VCE, and prior Lumenary ledgers, this idea says grain is only the predictive surplus of a prior dissolution target after rehearsed operation and receiving claimant grammar have already explained what they can.
